schema.appDescription: Calculate income tax surcharge and health & education cess for high-income individuals. FY 2026-27 rates.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Income Tax Surcharge Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown table to see how your numbers change over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust the inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option for your situation.

faq.q: What is income tax surcharge in India?
faq.a: Surcharge is an additional tax on income tax itself, levied on high-income taxpayers. For FY 2026-27: income ₹50L–₹1Cr attracts 10% surcharge; ₹1Cr–₹2Cr attracts 15%; ₹2Cr–₹5Cr attracts 25%; above ₹5Cr attracts 37% (new regime capped at 25% post Budget 2023).

faq.q: What is health and education cess?
faq.a: 4% cess is levied on (income tax + surcharge) for all taxpayers. It funds health and education initiatives. There is no exemption from cess.

faq.q: Is surcharge applicable under the new tax regime?
faq.a: Yes, surcharge applies under both old and new tax regimes. However, the maximum surcharge under the new regime for capital gains is capped at 15%.
Yes, the Surcharge Calculator on CalcPhi is completely free with no registration required.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We do not store any financial data.

faq.q: Can I use the Surcharge Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi is fully mobile-optimised and works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹75 lakh income — surcharge calculation
examples.text: For ₹75 lakh taxable income, base tax under new regime is approximately ₹15.6 lakh. Surcharge at 10% = ₹1.56 lakh. Cess at 4% on ₹17.16 lakh = ₹68,640. Total tax payable: ₹17.85 lakh (effective rate ~23.8%).

examples.heading: ₹2 crore income — 25% surcharge
examples.text: For ₹2 crore income, surcharge jumps to 25% of base tax. This significantly increases the effective tax rate. Use marginal relief provisions if income is just above the ₹1 crore or ₹2 crore threshold.
